reasoning: The resolution window is extremely narrow (approx. 5 weeks from the April 23, 2025 knowledge cutoff to the June 1, 2025 deadline). While Zimbabwe recently made an initial $3.1 million payment to a 'first batch' of 378 farms (out of 740 approved), the government has a long history of delaying and missing compensation payments (e.g., missing payments in Dec 2024). Crucially, the payment was heavily criticized as a "token gesture" and an April 23 report noted that the Compensation Steering Committee officially rejected the deal, demanding a renegotiation. Although a government spokesperson dismissed this rejection, the pushback introduces a reasonable risk of bureaucratic or political delay. Furthermore, economists characterized the April payment as a "publicity stunt" – which may have been timed for mid-April international events like the IMF/World Bank Spring Meetings to demonstrate progress on arrears. Without an impending catalyst in May, and considering the short timeline and active dispute, the likelihood of a subsequent 'second batch' payment or new tranche being fully executed before June 1 is low, estimated at ~22%.
